terms.definition: H(P, Q) = −Σ p(x) log q(x); expected surprise under Q when sampling from P. Canonical definition in this topic.
For one-hot true class y and predicted class probabilities π, loss = −log π_y; the multi-class ML objective derived from H(P, Q).
Cross-entropy for Bernoulli(p) and Bernoulli(q): −p log q − (1−p) log(1−q); used for binary classification with sigmoid outputs.
Synonym for cross-entropy loss in classification; negative log-likelihood of the true label under the predicted distribution.
Cross-entropy where Q is parameterized by softmax(logits); standard output layer for multi-class neural networks.
Informal name emphasizing H(P, Q) as entropy plus KL excess; same quantity as cross-entropy.
